metadata.title: Claude, Unsupervised
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream; code stays Onda dark glass, never re-skinned
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Turkish. Wagwan check: charsum('claude-unsupervised') % 10 == 2, not 0.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: inner beats pair the spark with one short serif line; typing is B00-only. OUTRO LAW: outro restates the title. Spine: B00 ASK · B01 THE FLAG (Onda code-block on claude-window blank view) · B02–B06 one lesson per beat (fear, reframe, the video case, the loop, the routing tie-in) · B07 VERDICT (artifact view: the seatbelt rules) · B08 title-restate outro. Premise from Bear: video pipelines are the safe case for --dangerously-skip-permissions — bad output is regenerable, it runs until done, human reviews at the end; and it's Opus running the loop, not Fable. The video must carry the warning honestly: the flag is only as safe as the blast radius. HANDOFF LAW: second-to-last beat = composer with a paste-ready prompt for the viewer (greeting 'Your turn.'); typing appears in cold open and handoff only.

beats.narration_text: Thanks Bear, let's recap with Claude. Unsupervised means every permission prompt is gone; Claude executes unattended from start to finish. Nothing about the flag is safe by default: it can write, delete, or install anything in reach. Danger is a property of what's in reach, so ask what the worst thing in the folder is. Safety comes from git-tracked folders, regenerable outputs, no secrets, and pipeline gates needing a human signature.

artifactLines: Unsupervised means every permission prompt is gone; Claude executes unattended from start to finish.
Nothing about the flag is safe by default: it can write, delete, or install anything in reach.
Danger is a property of what's in reach, so ask what the worst thing in the folder is.
Safety comes from git-tracked folders, regenerable outputs, no secrets, and pipeline gates needing a human signature.

beats.narration_text: The reframe that danger isn't a property of the flag but of what's in reach is what I want to operationalize before I ever run unattended. Using the video pipeline example where a bad beat is just a rerenderable file, help me audit my own setup. Give me: (1) the worst-case question I should ask about my folder, (2) how to place gates so the run can't spend credits or publish without my signature, (3) a one-page checklist matching the git-tracked, regenerable-outputs, no-secrets rules.

props.command: The reframe that danger isn't a property of the flag but of what's in reach is what I want to operationalize before I ever run unattended. Using the video pipeline example where a bad beat is just a rerenderable file, help me audit my own setup. Give me: (1) the worst-case question I should ask about my folder, (2) how to place gates so the run can't spend credits or publish without my signature, (3) a one-page checklist matching the git-tracked, regenerable-outputs, no-secrets rules.

beats.narration_text: It means what it says. Every 'may I run this?', every 'may I write that?' — gone. Claude executes, unattended, start to finish. That is the whole feature.

props.title: zsh — unsupervised
props.code:
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions \
  "build the reel: audio, conform, render, QC"

beats.narration_text: Be clear about the trade. Unattended means it can write anything, delete anything, install anything — in reach. Nothing about this flag is safe by default.
But danger isn't a property of the flag. It's a property of what's in reach. Wrong question: is the flag safe. Right question: what's the worst thing in this folder?
Videos are my case. A bad beat isn't a disaster — it's a file. Rerender B03, keep the rest. When the worst case is a re-run, a permission prompt is just a slower render.
So the pipeline runs and runs until it's done — audio, conform, render, QC. I show up at the end like an editor, not a babysitter. Check the cut, ask for changes, rerender those beats.
And the model grinding unattended? Not my flagship. Fable wrote the skill; Opus runs it. Unsupervised is the run-low half of author high, run low.

The rules, one page. Git-tracked folder. Outputs you can regenerate. No secrets in reach. Gates inside the pipeline — mine can't spend audio credits or publish without a human signature. Fail any one of those? Keep the prompts. They're the seatbelt.

artifactLines: Regenerable outputs — the worst case is a re-render.
Nothing secret in reach — no credentials, no prod, nothing unique.
Gates inside the pipeline — audio spend and publishing still need a human.
Fail any one? Keep the permission prompts. They're the seatbelt.

beats.narration_text: Before you flip that flag — your turn. Take this prompt and make Claude check your folder against the seatbelt rules.

props.command: does this folder pass the seatbelt rules for --dangerously-skip-permissions? AskUserQuestion.
